name: Karma
description: Test runner for JavaScript that spawns real browsers to execute tests, popular for Angular projects although now in maintenance mode.

seo.title: Karma — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: Karma: Test runner for JavaScript that spawns real browsers to execute tests, popular for Angular projects although now in maintenance mode.

when_to_use: Run JavaScript tests in real Chrome, Firefox, Safari
Angular unit test execution with Karma test runner
Continuous test re-run on file change during development
Browser-compatible test execution with coverage reports

pros: Runs tests in real browsers, not just jsdom
Works with Jasmine, Mocha and Chai
Good CI integration for cross-browser runs
cons: Slow to boot compared to Vitest or Jest
Angular team now pushes Web Test Runner
review.verdict: A legacy default for Angular that teams should actively plan to migrate off of.
review.best_for: Existing Angular codebases not yet ready to switch test runners
review.not_for: Any new project, Vitest or Web Test Runner are better picks

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free / hobby tier; no peak-lock evidence.
